name: North American School Bus LLC
owner_or_parent: North America Central School Bus / H.I.G. Capital
principals: Steve Hemmerlein (former CEO, retired); Jason Walker (current CEO)
districts_or_customers: Aurora-area IL districts (Illinois Central School Bus subsidiary facility)
recent_news: NACSB is owned by H.I.G. Capital (acquired by H.I.G. Infrastructure 2018, exit from Goldner Hawn); 5th largest school bus operator in NA with 6,000+ buses across 46 facilities
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. The Montgomery, IL location at 2020 Albright Rd is a facility of Illinois Central School Bus, a subsidiary of NACSB (H.I.G. Capital portfolio company). Steve Hemmerlein is the NACSB CEO, not an independent owner. Competitor platform to Summit.

name: S & S School Bus Garage
owner_or_parent: unknown (likely S&S Consolidated ISD in-house transportation)
districts_or_customers: S&S Consolidated ISD (Sadler/Southmayd, TX); district has ~990 students
recent_news: No M&A news found; address (400 Elementary Dr) is on S&S Consolidated ISD campus, strongly suggesting this is the district's in-house bus garage rather than an independent contractor
fit_notes: Likely NOT a tuck-in target. Address co-locates with S&S Consolidated ISD; appears to be a district-owned in-house transportation operation, not a privately-owned contractor. Small district (~990 students) limits scale even if private. Needs phone verification.

name: Hoyles School Transportation
principals: Franklin 'Frank' Hoyle (President, deceased April 2020); Norma Hoyle (co-owner, deceased December 2021)
districts_or_customers: Historically served Pekin/Tazewell County IL area schools (Pekin District 108, 303)
recent_news: Frank and Norma Hoyle owned/operated Hoyle Transportation for 43 years and RETIRED in June 2010; Frank died April 13, 2020; Norma died December 3, 2021. Entity may be dormant/wound down
fit_notes: NOT a viable tuck-in target. Owners retired in 2010 and both founders are deceased. Lead-exec data appears stale. Worth a phone check to confirm wind-down status, but this is almost certainly a closed/legacy business. Pekin transportation has since shifted (Pekin city ending school bus service by July 2026).

name: Bradley Bus Inc
districts_or_customers: Likely serves Bend-La Pine Schools or surrounding Deschutes County (OR) districts
recent_news: No M&A or news coverage found; very low online footprint. Bend-La Pine district transports ~6,000 students daily with mostly in-house ops + some contractors
fit_notes: Possible tuck-in target if confirmed independent. Family-owned single-location operator in La Pine, OR with ~58 employees implies ~25-40 buses. Gene Bradley as president (likely 2nd/3rd gen) suggests potential succession opportunity. Needs FMCSA/SOS verification and direct outreach to confirm size, contracts, and ownership status.

name: John Leckie Inc
principals: Three grandchildren of founder John Leckie operate the company (specific names not disclosed publicly)
districts_or_customers: Hackensack Public Schools, Ridgefield Public Schools, Torah Academy of Bergen County, South Bergen Jointure Commission, Region II Pascack Valley Council for Special Education; 70+ schools in northern NJ
recent_news: No M&A or sale news; active blog through Feb 2023; family touts 60+ year history. Maintains two shop locations and full-time mechanics
fit_notes: STRONG tuck-in candidate. Independent, 3rd-generation family business since 1958 with 100+ buses, 150 employees, diversified contract base across Bergen County NJ districts including SPED (high-margin). Dense urban/suburban NJ footprint complements Summit's Northeast presence. Third-gen ownership = realistic succession window. Worth direct outreach.
